title: QUANTUM FLUCTUATION?
journal: Liberating Science: The Early Universe, Evolution and the Public Voice of Science
abstract:
Abstract
               ‘Quantum fluctuation’ has entered into common language, but what does it mean? It is widely taken to mean something untrue. It is widely taken to mean that the vacuum of space is a kind of froth of randomly appearing and disappearing particles or chaotic ripples. But in fact the vacuum is smooth in both space and time and no particles spontaneously appear in it. The term ‘fluctuation’ is misleading. The physics here is really about a smooth spread of values. This is expounded through various examples and analogies.
